Iran suspends agreement with Washington after 'US violations'
articles.summary: Iran suspends agreement with Washington after 'US violations' Iran's deputy foreign minister, Kazem Gharibabadi, said Tehran had suspended its commitments to the memorandum of understanding (MoU) with Washington as US attacks continue.  “The US has violated and suspended all its commitments within the framework of the Islamabad MoU,” Gharibabadi said. The deputy foreign minister added that Tehran had suspended its commitments under the agreement as it is "busy defending the country".

articles.title: Kuwait reschedules flights due to airspace closures
articles.summary: Kuwait reschedules flights due to airspace closures Kuwait Airways said it is rescheduling some flights due to airspace closures, according to a statement posted on social media. Similar disruptions occurred during the regional escalation earlier this year, when airspace closures across several Gulf countries caused widespread travel disruptions, affecting hundreds of thousands of passengers. Iran’s Tasnim agency quoted Hamzehpour as referring to the US strikes as “a series of crimes and terrorist attacks”. Citing the data released by Iran, MarineTraffic said that in the three weeks following the 17 June US-Iran peace memorandum, India-bound ships accounted for 20 percent of outbound transit requests, second only to China at 21 percent. articles.summary: Iran media: Blasts in Khorramabad, US assets in Bahrain struck Iranian media have reported that US missile strikes launched from Kuwait caused explosions in the city of Khorramabad in Lorestan province. Here are some other developments on Saturday: 'Bahrain targets hit': According to an Iranian military statement reported by the Tasnim news agency, Iranian missiles targeted US military assets and fuel tanks at the Sheikh Isa airbase and several communication bridges in Bahrain. The statement said the US had used the base to launch “operations against regional targets, especially our country”.
